title: Twin Brothers and a Legendary City
story: Legend tells of twin brothers who changed history. Romulus and Remus, raised by a wolf, founded a mighty city in 753 BC. That city? Rome! On April 21, they chose a hill to build their new home. But the brothers argued, and Romulus became the sole founder of what would grow into one of the world's greatest empires.
activity_question: Who were the legendary founders of Rome?
bonus_fact: The she-wolf who raised the twins is still a symbol of Rome today.

title: A Grand Cathedral's Sacred Moment
story: Bells rang out as the Santiago de Compostela Cathedral was finally consecrated in 1211. After over a century of construction, this magnificent church dedicated to St. James stood complete. King Alfonso IX of Leon attended the ceremony on April 21, marking the end of a journey that began in 1075. Pilgrims from far and wide would now have a grand destination for their travels.

title: Sliding into Safety: NYC's First Fireman's Pole
story: Whoosh! In 1878, New York City firefighters got a speedy new way to reach their trucks. On April 21, the city installed its first fireman's pole. This clever invention helped brave firefighters slide down quickly from their upstairs living quarters to the ground floor, ready to zoom off and save lives. It was a big change from running down stairs!
activity_question: What did New York City install for its firefighters?
follow_up_question: Why do you think a fireman's pole might be faster than using stairs?
